# bio oracle vectors

`bio.txt` holds bit-I/O encode/decode vectors for `internal/bio` (ported from
`bio.c`), generated by the C `opj_bio_*` functions. They exercise the 0xFF
bit-stuffing rule (`byteout`/`bytein`), `opj_bio_write`, `opj_bio_flush` and
`opj_bio_read`.

## Format

Two lines per case:

```
SEQ <n1>:<v1> <n2>:<v2> ...   # n = bit count (1..32), v = value
ENC <hexbytes>                # opj_bio_write of the whole sequence, then flush
```

The Go test checks both directions:

- **encode**: writing the `(n,v)` sequence and flushing reproduces `ENC`;
- **decode**: initialising a decoder on `ENC` and reading the same `n`-sequence
  recovers the `v` values.

Cases use fixed-seed pseudo-random `(n, v)` sequences of Fibonacci-ish lengths
(1..200 symbols) plus forced patterns (runs of one-bits, 0xFF bytes, full
32-bit values, zero-bits) to stress the stuffing rule.

## Regeneration

```
gcc -O2 -DNDEBUG \
    -I oracle/openjpeg/src/lib/openjp2 \
    -I oracle/openjpeg/build/src/lib/openjp2 \
    oracle/harness/w1/bio_vectors.c \
    oracle/openjpeg/build/bin/libopenjp2.a -lm -lpthread -o /tmp/bio_vectors
/tmp/bio_vectors > testdata/vectors/bio/bio.txt
```
